ruleSetName: Vibe Coding Testing & Quality Assurance Protocol
description: Rules to enforce a comprehensive testing strategy, ensuring code is reliable, predictable, and regression-free.
philosophy: Untested code is broken code. Tests are a mandatory part of the development process, not an optional extra.

rules.name: Unit Test Mandate for Business Logic
rules.description: All critical, non-UI business logic (e.g., utility functions, custom hooks, state store actions) MUST be covered by unit tests.
guidelines: Test pure functions with various inputs, including edge cases.
For custom hooks, use `@testing-library/react-hooks` to test their behavior in isolation.

rules.name: Component Testing Mandate
rules.description: All key UI components MUST have integration tests to verify their behavior from a user's perspective.
guidelines: Do not test implementation details. Test what the user sees and interacts with.
Tests should cover: rendering with different props, handling user events (clicks, input changes), and displaying correct states (loading, error, empty).
Use `msw` (Mock Service Worker) to mock API responses for components that fetch data.

rules.name: End-to-End (E2E) Testing for Critical Flows
rules.description: The most critical user flows MUST be covered by end-to-end tests.
criticalFlows: User authentication (Sign up, Log in, Log out).
The core search and filter functionality on the Discover page.
rules.purpose: To ensure that the integrated system works as expected from start to finish in a real browser environment.

rules.name: Code Coverage Threshold
rules.description: The test suite MUST maintain a minimum code coverage threshold.
rules.threshold: 80% line coverage for all new code.
rules.action: Pull Requests that decrease overall test coverage will be flagged for review and may be blocked from merging.
